title: chore: remove dormant /play iframe embed machinery
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
Removes the `/play` iframe embed/auth/theme machinery (from #11800 + #11807). The iframe host that consumes it ships only in the still-open website rebuild (#11792), so none of this has a live consumer on `main` today — the website PR can reintroduce exactly what it needs.

- **sdk**: drop the `polli-embed` bridge (`embed.tsx`/`embed-protocol.ts` + tests), the `EmbedBridge` auto-mount in `PolliProvider`, frame-local memory storage, and the host-login reroute
- **ui**: drop `useHostThemeSync` (host→iframe theme `postMessage`) and `AppUserMenu` host-capability self-hiding
- **ui**: drop the cross-subdomain color-mode cookie → back to per-origin `localStorage` (also removes the cookie read in the 5 pre-paint scripts)
- **apps**: drop `useHostThemeSync` calls in playground/websim

**Kept**: `isEmbeddedContext` cosmetic layout (#11791), the `localStorage` theme store + cross-tab sync.

**Net**: −1039 / +17 across 18 files.

Verified: sdk 30/30, ui 6/6, playground + websim typecheck against rebuilt packages, biome clean.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Sweeping up the iframe dust
gist.blurb: We're doing a little spring cleaning in the codebase, removing some dormant iframe embed machinery to keep things light and fast. Less code means fewer bugs for the hive!
gist.summary: Removed dormant `/play` iframe embed, auth, and theme sync machinery from the SDK and UI packages, shedding over 1,000 lines of unused code.
gist.impact: Developers will benefit from a cleaner, lighter codebase with the removal of the unused `polli-embed` bridge and cross-subdomain cookies.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee doing some spring cleaning, sweeping up old code blocks while the Nomnom creature eats the discarded iframe documents.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny bandana, sweeping up glowing pixelated code blocks with a small broom. The round tan Nomnom creature happily eats a stack of discarded iframe documents nearby. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and warm peach gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
